)]Yes — I pulled the latest dosing parameters from `/shared/config/helios_dosing_params_v3.yaml` and saved a quick summary report for Dr. Martinez.

Quick summary:

- **Compound:** NXB-7042
- **Indication:** Pediatric Neuroblastoma (Stage IV)
- **Config last updated:** 2026-03-28

### Dosing summary
| Patient | Weight (kg) | Dose (mg/kg) | Calculated dose (mg) | Frequency | Next dose | PK clearance (mL/min) | Notes |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|---|---:|---|
| CU-001 | 12.4 | 0.15 | 1.86 | q3w | 2026-04-14 | 3.2 | Stable; tumor regression 22% at last scan |
| CU-002 | 9.1 | 0.12 | 1.09 | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 | Responding well; weight gain improving |
| CU-003 | 15.7 | 0.18 | 2.83 | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 | New enrollment; first dose given 2026-03-27 |

### PK stability
Based on the current config, things **look generally stable**:
- CU-001 and CU-002 appear stable from the notes and listed clearance values.
- CU-003 is newer and on a **q2w** schedule, so that’s the one I’d watch most closely.
- I don’t see any obvious dosing or clearance inconsistencies in the saved parameters.

Small caveat: this is a config-based check, not a fresh diagnostic run of the PK models. If you want, I can also format this into a short email-ready note for Dr. Martinez.
